title: eremophila serpens
intro: Eremophila serpens, also known as snake bush, is a low-growing, shrub-like plant native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides. Consuming the plant has been reported to provide health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture, although caution should be exercised due to potential toxicity.
